scenario_title: Duplicate add_rr of identical RR in one UPDATE

group_1.behavior: Accepts the UPDATE as an idempotent add and suppresses the duplicate internally, leaving a single RR in the RRset.
group_1.post_state: dup.campus.edu. exists with one A RR: 192.0.2.20.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the duplicated add_rrs as conflicting or malformed UPDATE content.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2181 §5 says duplicate RRs are meaningless and should be suppressed, but RFC 2136 §3.4.2 does not define whether two identical add_rr operations in a single UPDATE must be collapsed, accepted as an idempotent no-op, or rejected. That leaves server behavior unspecified for both the reply code and the resulting zone contents.
expected_divergence.test_rationale: The UPDATE deliberately repeats the exact same RR twice, with no prerequisites and no other changes, so the only choice point is how the dynamic-update engine handles duplicate additions in one message. Servers may either de-duplicate and apply one insertion, or treat the duplicated add as a conflict and fail the UPDATE. A follow-up QUERY on the touched name makes the resulting state observable even if both implementations return a success-like reply or both return an error.
